macro_context.market_sentiment: Broadly bullish as of May 1, 2026. S&P 500 closed at a fresh all-time high of 7,230.12 (+0.29%) and Nasdaq hit a record 25,114.44 (+0.89%), driven by Apple earnings beat and AI trade momentum. April was the best month for both indices since 2020.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026, with an unusually contentious 8-4 vote split — the most dissents since October 1992. Elevated inflation from energy prices (Middle East conflict) keeps rate cuts off the table near-term; Powell fighting to remain Fed Chair via legal challenge against Trump.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict threatening Strait of Hormuz closure is driving a ~19% forecast rise in energy commodity prices, feeding inflation. Iran peace proposal on May 1 offered brief market relief. Additional flashpoints: US-Venezuela tensions, NATO posture in Greenland, broad tariff uncertainty. IMF adverse scenario projects global growth of 2.5% with 5.4% inflation.
key_events: Fed next meeting: timing uncertain, no rate cuts expected near-term given elevated inflation
Iran peace talks developing — outcome could materially reduce oil price pressure
Q1 2026 earnings season winding down with broadly strong results (tech leading)
Tariff impacts on consumer goods and supply chains remain a live risk for apparel/footwear

ticker_news.headline: Nike cutting ~1,400 jobs as part of 'Win Now' restructuring amid disappointing sales and margin pressures in Greater China
Nike stock trades at ~$44.35, trading near multi-year lows; Motley Fool asks 'Nike Stock Looks Cheap. But Is It?'
CEO Elliott Hill and Apple CEO Tim Cook both made significant personal share purchases in Nike, signaling insider confidence
Nike Zoom Hyperflight and other new sneaker releases scheduled for May 2026, part of ongoing product refresh
Wave of analyst downgrades and price target cuts in April 2026 drove NKE to multiple new 52-week lows

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Heavy wave of April 2026 downgrades and PT cuts: CICC downgraded to market perform (PT $58 from $69); DA Davidson cut buy to neutral (PT $46 from $72); Wells Fargo kept overweight but cut PT to $55 from $65; UBS neutral at $54 from $58; Citigroup neutral at $53 from $65. Last upgrade was Barclays raising PT to $73 on March 11, 2026. Overall consensus remains Moderate Buy (13 buy, 11 hold, 0 sell from 24 analysts) with avg PT $63.44 — implying ~43% upside from current ~$44.35 price, but recent momentum is decidedly negative.

web_context.competitor_activity: Adidas is the most significant competitive threat: CEO Bjorn Gulden is ahead of schedule on his 4-year turnaround plan, the Terrace shoe trend (Samba/Gazelle) has reclaimed the lifestyle market, and an Adidas-sponsored athlete (Sabastian Sawe) ran the first legal sub-2-hour marathon at the 2026 London Marathon — a major marketing coup over Nike. Hoka and On Running are taking premium performance market share. Under Armour is less of a threat, restructuring by parting with Steph Curry to save $45-50M and refocusing its brand.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings date: June 25, 2026 (after market close), reporting fiscal Q4 FY2026 (quarter ending May 31, 2026). Consensus EPS estimate: ~$0.12-$0.17 (sources vary). Revenue estimate: ~$10.86 billion. Results will be a key test of whether the 'Win Now' restructuring is showing tangible improvement in margins and Greater China sales.
